$comment: D-AS-REGALLOC-ARG-REGISTER-OCCUPIED (c75 correctness fix, variant 1 — GPR). The c75 reserve-K audit caught a SILENT MISCOMPILE: the reservation reserved an incoming argument register (x86_64 SysV r9 = the 6th int arg register) as spill-reload scratch, and the rewriter staged a reload through it at function entry, clobbering the 6th incoming parameter before its `arg` op read it (the 6th param read as the 5th -> 149 instead of 156). Root: the allocator/liveness never modelled an incoming ABI arg register as OCCUPIED over [entry, argOp.late) (the param vreg's range starts at its `arg` op; the mov-from-argreg is inserted POST-regalloc by callconv). FIX (lir_regalloc.cpp + lir_rewrite.cpp): (1) buildFreeLists reserves K caller-saved NON-ARG registers (excludes argGprs/argFprs/indirectResultRegister); (2) collectArgRegisterOccupied + a covered-window excludedScratch augment keeps the allocator off an arg register while a range live before its release could alias the incoming param; (3) rewriteOneFunc forbids the cc's arg registers as scratch for a spilled `arg`-op result. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the fix -> x86_64 returns 149 (WRONG). arm64 (~30 GPRs) never spills here (trivially correct, value-checked). probe(1..15): fx=6*7=42, rest=7+8+..+15=99, early=1+..+5=15 => 156.
